findings.description: Command injection: attacker-controlled job.payload values 'source'/'dest' are interpolated into a shell string and executed via subprocess.run(shell=True), enabling arbitrary OS command execution (CWE-78, OWASP A03:2021 Injection).

Root cause of the injection: f-string cmd = f"convert {src} {dest}" builds a shell command from untrusted payload with no shlex.quote/escaping; fix by passing an argument list (e.g. ["convert", src, dest]) with shell=False and validating src/dest.

Availability/DoS: run_with_retries resets attempt=0 on line 69 every iteration, so the while attempt < MAX_RETRIES loop never terminates when execute_job persistently returns False or raises, hanging the worker indefinitely on a single (possibly malicious) job (CWE-835 / CWE-400).

DoS via event-loop stall: blocking time.sleep() inside async poll_loop (instead of await asyncio.sleep) freezes the entire service event loop on every empty poll, degrading availability of all co-hosted coroutines (primarily a logic/async-misuse bug — defer detail to sibling critics).

Missing input validation/allow-listing on job kinds and payload schema means malformed/untrusted jobs reach the shell path with no defense-in-depth; consider validating job.kind and payload types before dispatch.
